title: The Purpose Of Unit 01s Horn
wikitext:
[]: The Purpose Of Unit 01's Horn             Quote Post by Ray » Fri Aug 21, 2015 4:43 pm I always found the Horn of Unit 01 to be a bit ridiculous. It's WAAAY too big. True it offers a nice instantly recognizable outline in profile, but what purpose does it serve to NERV? It doesn't seem to act as a counterbalance, it doesn't appear to offer any use in combat as a weapon though I imagine it would deliver one nasty headbutt, it seems to be there for pure Aesthetic purposes, which doesn't really make sense in universe. Why give the angels a handle to grab Unit 01 by? Why was it never broken off or damaged in combat (Lack of budget duh!) ? The Horns on Unit 02 in rebuild I see as much more practical, but the horn on Unit 01 still seems largely an impractical design choice for an apocalyptic death machine. Was it just there when they grew the armor from Angelic Tissue?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Aug 21, 2015 4:48 pm So it looks like an oni. The same way that grotesque masks were a feature of samurai armour.

[]:             Quote Post by Lord ikari shinji » Fri Aug 21, 2015 5:05 pm Unicorn bro, unicorn. Nuff said.

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Fri Aug 21, 2015 5:22 pm I still think it looks ridiculous!

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Aug 21, 2015 5:25 pm In both versions, when Unit-01 first goes berserk against Zeruel, the Eva pulls the Angel toward itself, butting heads with it while growling, and if you look closely at the shot you can see Unit-01's horn stabbing Zeruel through the eye. But yeah, the horn is primarily aesethtic. Along with the tall, slender bodies, the horn gives of the impression of an oni, or demon, which contrasts nicely with the Angels. Adam as the giant of light has a horn, which implies the connection between itself and the Evas. Making Unit-01 more directly resemble Adam may have been a way to throw off the fact that it is actually a prodigy of Lilith; who they are trying to fool, however, I have no idea, since Seele seems pretty aware of Unit-01's true nature, at least by the time it matters to them in EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Fri Aug 21, 2015 5:32 pm The horns on 02 in Rebuild are 100% aesthetic because YAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA-MAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA-TOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOO

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Aug 21, 2015 6:11 pm See also: http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/5211/Unit-01-and-its-horn/ http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/3893/What-da-Hell-is-up-with-Unit-01s-Horn-thing/

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Aug 21, 2015 6:38 pm Sachi wrote: In both versions, when Unit-01 first goes berserk against Zeruel, the Eva pulls the Angel toward itself, butting heads with it while growling, and if you look closely at the shot you can see Unit-01's horn stabbing Zeruel through the eye.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Fri Aug 21, 2015 6:48 pm Sachi wrote: In both versions, when Unit-01 first goes berserk against Zeruel, the Eva pulls the Angel toward itself, butting heads with it while growling, and if you look closely at the shot you can see Unit-01's horn stabbing Zeruel through the eye.

[]:             Quote Post by IronEvangelion » Fri Aug 21, 2015 6:52 pm It's there for free condiments. Ritsuko knows that if you rub Unit 01's horn hard enough, you get magical evangelion mayonnaise. Which is what Gendo uses to make his sandwiches. Really though it's just there for the oni references and to make Unit 01 look cool. Let's face it, the eva is Barney purple, it needs all the help it can get in that department.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Fri Aug 21, 2015 6:53 pm So Yui's is bigger than Gendo's.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Fri Aug 21, 2015 6:54 pm IronEvangelion wrote: Ritsuko knows that if you rub Unit 01's horn hard enough, you get magical evangelion mayonnaise.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Fri Aug 21, 2015 9:19 pm Eva-02 gets its horns in Rebuild . And I didn't see any horns on Adam's Giant of Light form! Yes, Eva-01's horn is meant to invoke the Oni aesthetic, although there's a practical purpose as well - the horn has radio antennae built into it. When Eva-01 is swallowed by Leliel, the radio transmission cuts off only when the very tip of the horn goes into the shadow. Eva-00 has a lens-like antenna on top of its head, so it was likely that Nerv was experimenting with different sensor configurations on the Evas.

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Fri Aug 21, 2015 10:08 pm UrsusArctos wrote: Eva-02 gets its horns in Rebuild . And I didn't see any horns on Adam's Giant of Light form! Eva-00 has a lens-like antenna on top of its head, so it was likely that Nerv was experimenting with different sensor configurations on the Evas.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Fri Aug 21, 2015 10:11 pm UrsusArctos wrote: Yes, Eva-01's horn is meant to invoke the Oni aesthetic, although there's a practical purpose as well - the horn has radio antennae built into it.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Aug 21, 2015 10:43 pm Ray wrote: I Assumed that was a giant camera lens/eyeball.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Fri Aug 21, 2015 11:02 pm I realize that I may have quoted too much-- I was referring specifically to the radio antennae portion of Ursus's post, since I think there's more than enough sources that mention the design as creating some kind of Oni aesthetic. If your post still applies assuming that I was just talking about the radio part, wonderful!

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Aug 22, 2015 12:46 am Nuke : Yeah, I'm just referring to the antenna thing. I did skim through Sore o nasumono in case Yamashita scribbled down something, but there wasn't anything obvious. Nothing pointing at the horn and clearly announcing a function the way he does with Eva-00's cranial doodads. I was too lazy to carefully scan through all of the printed comments, though. Maybe later...

[]: Re: The Purpose Of Unit 01's Horn             Quote Post by amitakartok » Sat Aug 22, 2015 6:38 am Ray wrote: Why was it never broken off or damaged in combat?

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Sat Aug 22, 2015 11:32 am Hm. . . I guess I'll have to rewatch 1.0 to double check
